meta.notes: All words are built from the grammar rules in grammar.json. Repetition (XX, XXX) functions as punctuation and is noted where it occurs.

pairs.interpretation: The beginning opens. A threshold entered with force. The ox at the gate.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on doors leading to places of origin or power. Also a greeting: 'You have arrived at the threshold.'
pairs.grammarNotes: The simplest declarative fragment. Aleph opens; Dalet receives. No result glyph — the passage itself is the result.

pairs.interpretation: The passage leads to the beginning. To walk through is to become strong. A door that promises origin.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on doors leading to places of trial or renewal. 'Beyond this door, strength awaits.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-001. The door comes first — the passage is the subject, the destination is the result.

pairs.interpretation: The passage leads to the end. A door that seals. The threshold that, once crossed, cannot be uncrossed.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on doors that close permanently behind the traveler. Also a warning: 'What you enter, you will finish.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Dalet opens, Taw closes. A fragment of finality. The door itself is not the subject — the completion is.

pairs.interpretation: From the end, a door. Completion opens a new passage. The seal is not the end — it is a threshold to something else.
pairs.usage: Inscribed above the exit of a sealed chamber. 'You have finished. Now pass through.'
pairs.grammarNotes: The inversive pattern. Taw opens — time reverses. The end speaks the door.

pairs.interpretation: What begins with strength ends in completion. The ox plows the field; the furrow is the mark.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on tools, plows, and weapons. A blessing on work: 'May your strength find its mark.'
pairs.grammarNotes: No connector — strength reaches completion directly. The simplest arc: origin to end, with nothing between.

pairs.interpretation: The end renews the beginning. Completion is not exhaustion — it is the seed of new strength.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on graves and memorials. 'What is finished gives strength to what begins.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-005. The seal opens, and strength returns. An elegiac fragment.

pairs.interpretation: From the deep, power rises. The water births the ox. What is formless becomes strong.
pairs.usage: Inscribed near water sources, wells, and springs. 'The deep gives strength.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Mem as domain — unusual. The deep is not typically an origin, but here it is. Chaos as progenitor.

pairs.interpretation: Strength plunges into the deep. The ox enters the water. What is ordered submits to what is formless.
pairs.usage: A warning. 'Your strength will be swallowed.' Inscribed on doors leading to unknown depths.
pairs.grammarNotes: The inverse of pair-007. Strength as domain, chaos as destination — a descent.

pairs.interpretation: The hand builds what holds. Action creates structure. Make it stand.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on foundations and cornerstones. A builder's blessing.
pairs.grammarNotes: Imperative fragment. Yod commands; Samek receives. The simplest instruction: build.

pairs.interpretation: What holds is held by the hand. The structure depends on the builder. The pillar does not stand alone.
pairs.usage: A reminder of dependency. 'What supports you was made by someone.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-009. The pillar as subject — it stands because the hand made it.

pairs.interpretation: To divide is to strengthen. The cut separates the necessary from the excess — what remains is stronger.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on blades, pruning hooks, and surgical tools. 'Cut away weakness.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Zayin in opening position — rare. An imperative: divide, and strength follows.

pairs.interpretation: Cut a passage. Make a door where there was none. The blade opens the way.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on tools used to breach walls or clear paths. 'Make your own threshold.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Zayin opens, Dalet receives. The cut becomes a passage. Violence becomes architecture.

pairs.interpretation: The passage divides. To enter is to be changed. The door itself is a blade between two states.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on doors that lead to irrevocable transformations. 'You will not return as you were.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-012. The door as subject — it is the door that cuts.

pairs.interpretation: The deep opens. From chaos, a passage emerges. The water parts.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on doors that lead from order into mystery. 'Beyond: the unknown.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Mem as domain, Dalet as connector. The deep is not the destination — it is the origin of the door.

pairs.interpretation: The passage leads to the deep. To cross is to enter the unknown. The door opens onto water.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on doors leading to the sea, to wells, to the unconscious. 'What lies beyond is not solid.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Dalet opens, Mem receives. A door to chaos — one of the most feared inscriptions.

pairs.interpretation: Make a passage. The hand opens the way. Build the door, then walk through it.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on workshops and craft-halls. 'What you make opens the way.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Imperative: Yod commands the making; Dalet is what is made. Action produces passage.

pairs.interpretation: The passage acts. The door itself reaches out. The threshold is not passive — it shapes those who cross it.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on doors believed to change those who pass through. 'The door will work on you.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-016. The door as agent — it is the door that makes.

pairs.interpretation: What holds opens. The structure contains a passage. Even the pillar has a threshold.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on load-bearing walls that contain a door. 'The support does not block the way.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Samek as domain — the structure that also permits passage.

pairs.interpretation: The passage leads to support. To cross is to find what holds. The door opens onto stability.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on doors leading to sanctuaries, safe rooms, and places of refuge.
pairs.grammarNotes: Dalet opens, Samek receives. A door to safety — one of the most comforting inscriptions.

pairs.interpretation: The deep finds its seal. Chaos is completed. The water stills. The formless takes form.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on sealed vessels, tombs, and containers. 'What was chaotic is now contained.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Mem opens, Taw closes. A fragment of containment — the deep, sealed.

pairs.interpretation: The seal breaks. What was finished returns to the deep. Completion unmakes itself.
pairs.usage: A warning. 'What is sealed will not stay sealed.' Inscribed on tombs that should not be opened.
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-020. Taw opens — the seal is broken. Mem receives — chaos returns.

pairs.interpretation: Make the mark. The hand seals. Complete the work. Sign your name.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on finished works, signatures, and final statements. 'I made this. It is done.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Yod commands; Taw receives. The hand that seals — a maker's mark.

pairs.interpretation: The mark makes. Completion acts. What is finished continues to shape the world.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on legacies, testaments, and works that outlive their makers. 'The done thing still does.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-022. The seal as agent — the mark itself has hands.

pairs.interpretation: Cut the deep. Divide the waters. Bring form to the formless by separation.
pairs.usage: A creation fragment. 'Separate the waters.' Inscribed on tools of division used in ritual.
pairs.grammarNotes: Zayin opens, Mem receives. The cut that creates by dividing chaos.

pairs.interpretation: The deep divides. Chaos cuts itself. What is formless separates into what is.
pairs.usage: A cosmological fragment. 'The waters parted themselves.' Inscribed on creation texts.
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-024. The deep as agent of its own division.

pairs.interpretation: What holds is finished. The structure stands complete. The pillar reaches its mark.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on completed buildings, bridges, and monuments. 'It stands. It is done.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Samek opens, Taw closes. The pillar, sealed — a declaration of permanence.

pairs.interpretation: The mark supports. Completion becomes structure. What is finished holds up what comes next.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on foundations built on older ruins. 'The past holds the present.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-026. The seal as support — what is finished becomes the pillar.

pairs.interpretation: Take up the blade. The hand that divides. Make the cut.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on weapons and ritual knives. 'Your hand must do the cutting.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Two action glyphs — a rare structure. The hand commands; the cut executes. No result glyph — the cut is the result.

pairs.interpretation: The blade makes the hand. Division creates the doer. You become what you cut with.
pairs.usage: A warning about tools. 'The weapon shapes the wielder.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-028. The cut as agent — the blade that makes the hand.

pairs.interpretation: Power divides. Strength is not gentle — it separates, it cuts, it distinguishes.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on thrones, judges' seats, and places of authority. 'To rule is to divide.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Aleph opens, Zayin acts. Strength expressed as division.

pairs.interpretation: Carve the pillar. Shape the support. What is cut from the stone becomes what holds.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on quarried stone and carved columns. 'From the cut comes the support.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Zayin opens, Samek receives. The cut that produces structure.

pairs.interpretation: The structure divides. What holds also separates. The pillar is a boundary between one side and another.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on boundary markers and border stones. 'This pillar divides.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-031. The pillar as divider — what supports also separates.

pairs.interpretation: Power reaches out. Strength becomes action. The ox is yoked — it pulls.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on yokes, harnesses, and tools of labor. 'Strength, put to work.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Aleph opens, Yod acts. Strength channeled into making.

pairs.interpretation: The hand creates power. Making produces strength. Build, and become strong.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on forges and workshops. 'What you make makes you.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-033. The hand as origin of strength.

pairs.interpretation: The deep acts. Chaos reaches out. What is formless shapes something.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on places of inspiration and madness. 'The deep has hands.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Mem as domain, Yod as action. A rare and troubling fragment — chaos with agency.

pairs.interpretation: Make the deep. Shape chaos. The hand that stirs the water — creation as disturbance.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on potters' wheels, painters' palettes, and artists' tools. 'Create from the formless.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-035. The hand that enters chaos to shape it.

pairs.interpretation: Power becomes structure. The ox supports the roof. What is strong becomes what holds.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on load-bearing columns and structural supports. 'Strength, made to stand.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Aleph opens, Samek receives. The transformation of force into form.

pairs.interpretation: The structure empowers. What holds also strengthens. The pillar does not just stand — it enables.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on places of rest and recovery. 'What holds you makes you strong.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-037. The pillar as source of strength.

pairs.interpretation: Finish the cut. Complete the division. The final separation — what is cut away does not return.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on final judgments, exiles, and partings. 'It is severed. It is done.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Zayin opens, Taw closes. The cut that seals — a fragment of finality.

pairs.interpretation: The seal divides. Completion is not the end — it is a blade that separates what was from what will be.
pairs.usage: Inscribed on endings that are also beginnings. 'What is finished cuts you free.'
pairs.grammarNotes: Inverted pair-039. The seal as divider — completion that liberates.
